edges.description: Excess SETBP1 alters transcriptional control over developmental programs
Dysregulated SETBP1 activity disrupts brain development
Impaired neurogenesis leads to profound developmental and cognitive disability
Altered brain development contributes to the syndrome's severe seizure phenotype

nodes.description: SETBP1 acts as an epigenetic hub at AT-rich promoter regions to activate developmental genes, and pathogenic variants dysregulate this transcriptional program.

biological_processes: regulation of transcription by RNA polymerase II

evidence.reference_title: SETBP1 induces transcription of a network of development genes by acting as an epigenetic hub.
evidence.snippet: Here we show that SETBP1 binds to gDNA in AT-rich promoter regions, causing activation of gene expression through recruitment of a HCF1/KMT2A/PHF8 epigenetic complex.

evidence.reference_title: SETBP1 induces transcription of a network of development genes by acting as an epigenetic hub.
evidence.snippet: Gene ontology analysis of deregulated SETBP1 target genes indicates that they are also key controllers of visceral organ development and brain morphogenesis.

nodes.description: Classical Schinzel-Giedion syndrome is caused by degron-region SETBP1 variants that disrupt normal protein degradation and produce toxic accumulation.

evidence.reference_title: Cell-type-specific gene expression and regulation in the cerebral cortex and kidney of atypical Setbp1(S858R) Schinzel Giedion Syndrome mice.
evidence.snippet: SETBP1 variants causing classical SGS cluster at the degron, disrupting SETBP1 protein degradation and resulting in toxic accumulation, while those located outside cause milder atypical SGS.

nodes.description: Pathogenic SETBP1 activity disrupts neurogenesis, neuronal migration, and cell-type-specific regulatory programs in the brain.

evidence.reference_title: SETBP1 induces transcription of a network of development genes by acting as an epigenetic hub.
evidence.snippet: In line with these findings, in utero brain electroporation of mutated SETBP1 causes impairment of mouse neurogenesis with a profound delay in neuronal migration.

nodes.description: Severe epilepsy is a major downstream neurologic consequence of Schinzel- Giedion syndrome.

evidence.snippet: Classic Schinzel-Giedion syndrome (SGS), an ultra-rare multisystem disorder caused by gain-of-function pathogenic variants in a SETBP1 mutational hot spot, is characterized by global neurodevelopmental impairment leading to moderate-to-profound intellectual disability, epilepsy (often refractory to treatment), hypotonia, spasticity, dysautonomia, hearing loss, and cerebral visual impairment.
Classic Schinzel-Giedion syndrome (SGS), an ultra-rare multisystem disorder caused by gain-of-function pathogenic variants in a SETBP1 mutational hot spot
SETBP1 | HGNC:15573 | Schinzel-Giedion syndrome | MONDO:0010010 | AD | Definitive

nodes.description: Downstream consequences of dysregulated SETBP1 activity include severe global developmental impairment and intellectual disability.

evidence.snippet: Classic Schinzel-Giedion syndrome (SGS), an ultra-rare multisystem disorder caused by gain-of-function pathogenic variants in a SETBP1 mutational hot spot, is characterized by global neurodevelopmental impairment leading to moderate-to-profound intellectual disability, epilepsy (often refractory to treatment), hypotonia, spasticity, dysautonomia, hearing loss, and cerebral visual impairment.
